decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to enhance lateral heat spreading and reduce the thermal gradient across the chip. I will increase the spreader conductivity by a factor of 1.5 (from 450 to 675), but since the upper bound is 500, I will set it to the maximum allowed value of 500.0 W/(m*K).

changes.reason: Maximizing spreader conductivity improves lateral heat distribution, directly lowering the chip's peak temperature towards the 85°C constraint.

expected_effects: Improved feasibility status regarding thermal constraints
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur as conductivity approaches the upper bound; if this single change is insufficient, geometric changes (width/height) will be required in the next step.
